selection_policy.smoke: Use the smallest stable text model to validate environments and benchmark harness.
selection_policy.single_gpu: Prefer models that fit within one RTX 3080 Ti 12GB with practical context lengths.
selection_policy.dual_gpu: Use a larger model to evaluate tensor-parallel scaling across two consumer GPUs.

models.notes: Fastest cold-start smoke test model. Used to validate environment creation, server startup, health checks, and request path correctness before running larger workloads.
Primary single-GPU reference model. Small enough for stable latency and concurrency sweeps on a 12GB card while still being meaningful for practical serving comparisons.

Latest small dense Qwen3.5 checkpoint available locally. Preferred smoke and low-latency representative model because it is newer than the legacy Qwen3 0.6B/1.7B entries while remaining easy to fit on a single RTX 3080 Ti 12GB.
Older fallback dense stress model kept for compatibility. It is no longer the first recommendation once newer Qwen3.5 0.8B and 9B checkpoints are available locally.
Latest larger dense Qwen3.5 checkpoint available locally. Chosen as the main dual-GPU dense benchmark target because it is newer and more representative than the existing Qwen3 8B entry.

Latest local MoE representative checkpoint. It is intentionally included even though dual consumer GPUs may be insufficient, because success or failure on startup is itself a meaningful benchmark result for local deployment feasibility.
Alternative 4B-class instruct checkpoint. Kept as a fallback if the non-instruct 4B model shows tokenizer or chat-template issues in either framework.
Primary tensor-parallel scaling model for 2x RTX 3080 Ti. Used to compare startup reliability, communication overhead, and throughput scaling across both frameworks.
